articles.title: LOADING PYDANTIC MODELS FROM JSON WITHOUT RUNNING OUT OF MEMORY
articles.summary: Memory usage can become a significant bottleneck when working with large JSON files and Pydantic. The default Pydantic JSON loading method can result in a 20x memory multiplier, making it impractical for large files. To reduce memory consumption, it's better to instead use ijson, an incremental JSON parser, to stream the JSON data instead of loading it all at once.

articles.title: IN 2025, APPLE STILL MAKES IT HARD TO PLAY YOUR OWN MP3S, SO I WROTE MY OWN APP
articles.summary: Frustrated with Apple's limitations on playing personal MP3s and the subscription-based models of third-party apps, this developer built his own iOS music player with features like full-text search and iCloud support using Swift and SwiftUI. It has a local-first experience and uses SQLite for fast fuzzy searching.

articles.title: EVERYTHING'S A BUG (OR AN ISSUE)
articles.summary: Treating every task as a bug is an effective approach to get things done. This method is built upon four key principles: all tasks are bugs, a universal schema, a single assignee, and flexible queries. Modern tools like GitHub Issues fall short of these principles, leading to project management frustrations.

articles.title: WHY PROPERTY TESTING FINDS BUGS UNIT TESTING DOES NOT
articles.summary: Property-Based Testing (PBT) is great at uncovering bugs that unit tests miss due to the combinatorial explosion of edge cases in complex systems. While unit tests focus on specific inputs, PBT uses randomized inputs to test general properties, revealing unexpected interactions between multiple variables. The advantage of PBT increases with the complexity of the input space, such as multiple integer inputs or nested data structures, where manual identification of edge cases becomes too hard.

articles.title: WHEN A TEAM IS TOO BIG
articles.summary: A team that is too big has inefficient stand-up meetings and a lack of shared understanding due to specialized roles and tasks. Such teams work better after transitioning to a generalist model, where members broadened their skillsets and shared ownership across the entire development process.

articles.title: 🆕 SHOPIFY SHIPS MONSTER UPDATE TO ITS DEVELOPER PLATFORM, INCLUDING NEXT-GEN DEV PLATFORM
articles.summary: The newest update to Shopify lets developers build locally without tunnels, see realistic app previews on any plan, and use unified Polaris components across all surfaces. The sleek new developer platform [10] and CLI also includes declarative data definitions and isolated dev environments for faster builds. Discover 150+ updates in

articles.title: CLAUDE 4
articles.summary: Anthropic has released Claude 4, featuring two new models: Claude Opus 4, the world's best coding model, and Claude Sonnet 4, an upgrade with superior coding and reasoning abilities. These models have extended thinking with tool use, parallel tool execution, and improved memory capabilities when accessing local files.

articles.title: SQLITE-JS
articles.summary: SQLite-JS is an extension that lets you add JavaScript power to SQLite. You can create your own special functions (scalar, aggregate, and window types), define sorting rules (collation sequences), and even run JavaScript code directly inside your SQLite queries.

articles.title: ANNOUNCING TYPESCRIPT NATIVE PREVIEWS
articles.summary: TypeScript Native Previews is a port of the TypeScript compiler and toolset to native code. This native preview, eventually becoming TypeScript 7, includes initial support for JSX and JavaScript type checking, and editor features like completions, but still lacks some functionalities present in the current stable version, TypeScript 5.8.

articles.title: MCP EXPLAINED WITHOUT HYPE OR FLUFF
articles.summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) makes AI application integration easier by providing a standardized way for AI clients to access platform-specific data and tools through MCP Servers. These servers expose tools, prompts, resources, and sampling functionalities that AI clients can utilize without needing specific platform knowledge.

articles.title: THE “AI 2027” SCENARIO: HOW REALISTIC IS IT?
articles.summary: AI 2027 is a scenario that predicts the rapid arrival of superhuman AI and its dystopian consequences. However, the report relies heavily on speculation and unrealistic projections, and it doesn't have a solid scientific basis or consideration of alternative scenarios.

articles.title: DEVELOPERS SPEND MOST OF THEIR TIME FIGURING THE SYSTEM OUT
articles.summary: Developers spend most of their time figuring out existing systems, a costly and inefficient process primarily done through reading code.

articles.title: READING CODE IS STILL THE MOST EFFECTIVE METHOD TO DEBUG MULTI-THREAD BUG
articles.summary: Meticulously reading through code is still the most effective strategy for resolving complex multi-threading bugs.

articles.title: DEFUDDLE
articles.summary: Defuddle is a work-in-progress JavaScript library that extracts the main content from web pages by removing clutter and standardizing HTML elements for better readability and compatibility with tools like HTML-to-Markdown converters.

articles.title: ON 1,145 PULL REQUESTS PER DAY
articles.summary: A video from Stripe Sessions 2025 revealed that Stripe, with its strong engineering culture, averaged 1,145 production deployments per day in 2024 with minimal downtime, placing it in the top 1% of elite software delivery performers due to its heavy investment in automation, tooling, and engineer autonomy.
